rulings.Case Title: SAM GLEASON et al VS. SEEMA HOSSAIN et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 14, 2010, line 12, DEFENDANT STATE OF CALIFORNIA, ACTING BY AND THROUGH THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND SUMMARY ADJUDICATION MOTIONS ARE DENIED. AS TO ISSUE 1 (GENERAL NEGLIGENCE) A STATUTORY DUTY IS PROPERLY PLED UNDER HEALTH & SAFETY CODE SECTIONS 1596.608, 1596.605 AND 1596.62. AS TO THE 8TH CAUSE OF ACTION (NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATION) ISSUE 2 GOVERNMENT CODE SECTION 818.8 IMMUNITY IS NOT APPLICABLE TO NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATIONS INVOLVING THE RISK OF PHYSICAL HARM. GARCIA V. SUPERIOR COURT (1990) 50 CAL.3D 728. ISSUE 3: SUBSTANTIAL EVIDENCE OF MISREPRESENTATION EXISTS THAT HOSSAIN'S TRUSTLINE REGISTRY STATUS WAS CURRENT WHEN IN FACT, IT SHOULD HAVE BEEN FORFEITED. THE ACTIONABLE MISREPRESENTATION WAS MADE INDIRECTLY. SEE VARWIG V. ANDERSON (1977) 74 CAL.APP.3D 578. AS TO THE 10TH CAUSE OF ACTION (BREACH OF MANDATORY DUTY) ISSUE 4, A MANDATORY DUTY EXISTED TO AUTOMATICALLY FORFEIT TRUSTLINE REGISTRATION AND CONTINUALLY UPDATE THE REGISTRY. HEALTH & SAFETY CODE SECTIONS 1596.608, 1596.605 AND 1596.62. AS TO ISSUE 5, TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT RAISED WHETHER DEFENDANT'S ACTIONS WERE THE PROXIMATE CAUSE OF INJURY, SINCE HOSSAIN WAS IN FACT REGISTERED AND VERIFICATION BY NELSON WOULD NOT HAVE PREVENTED HER FROM PLACING THE CHILD IN HOSSAIN'S CARE. AS TO THE 6TH ISSUE, A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT IS RAISED AS TO EXACTLY HOW AND WHEN THE CHILD WAS INJURED. THE COURT DECLINES TO RULE ON ISSUES 7 AND 8 AS THEY DO NOT DISPOSE OF A CAUSE OF ACTION. CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 437c(f)(1). =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT S. VAILE VS. DELOITTE & TOUCHE, LLP
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Action With Prejudice, For Mootness As To All Defendants Established By The Law Of The Case On Appeal Of This Action; Or Alternatively By Renewed Demurrer And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings Request For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 14, 2010, line 2, DEFENDANT MARSHAL WILLICK, MARSHAL S. WILLICK, P.C., A NEVADA PROFESSIONAL Motion To Dismiss Action With Prejudice, For Mootness As To All Defendants Established By The Law Of The Case On Appeal Of This Action; Or Alternatively By Renewed Demurrer And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings. DENIED. THE RECORD IS NOT ENTIRELY CLEAR WHETHER THE COURT OF APPEAL FOUND THAT THIS CASE IS MOOT. THE COURT OF APPEAL'S ORDER IS AMBIGUOUS. DEFENDANTS ARGUE THAT THE NEVADA SUPREME COURT DISMISSED PLAINTIFF'S APPEAL, BUT THE DISMISSAL ORDER IS FROM CASE #55911. THE NEVADA SUPREME COURT STAY ORDER IS FROM CASE #55446 AND THAT IS THE CASE THAT SEEKS REVIEW OF TRIAL COURT'S RULING REGARDING THE CALIFORNIA CLAIMS. =(302/CWW)
